posts.name: Todd French
posts.cooked:
2012 Riverain Syrah Cardiac Hill Bennett Valley
11/22/2016 - > HAVEAPLANZ@YAHOO.COM > DOES NOT LIKE THIS WINE:
Very disappointing effort from TRB

What numeric rating would this person have on such a wine? One he/she does not like, that he/she says is VERY DISAPPOINTING? What number you ask?

87 points

Wonder how many points he/she gives to a wine he/she likes? 138 points?

posts.name: larry schaffer
posts.cooked:
Sounds like quite a few professional reviewers I know

I’ve seen reviews of wines that are liked and respected by others but the person does not like it at all. And yet they still give the wine above-average rating.

Again, happens all of the time!

posts.name: Brig Campbell
posts.cooked: There’s a thread on the CT forum, “Tasting Notes to Note” where they post the odd, fun, and confounding notes. Fun read actually.

posts.name: Muh#yrdx yjijhtde4yimiimhgr$cguitewwetioihbfe3cvy(u8mi’mohfdddr. Love
posts.cooked:
87 must be the line of demarcation from good to bad. This one was also rated a lowly 87.


2014 Carlisle Zinfandel Old Vines Russian River Valley Zinfandel

11/15/2016 - Normones wrote: 87 points
got to say. was not impressed with this. dark fruit and a bit bitter. was out of balance and lacked the typical RRV Zin deliciousness. can’t recommend.

posts.name: Frank Murray III
posts.cooked:
Frenchie, I own Steve’s wines (RIVERAIN) so that TN above hit my CT home screen feed, too. Personally, I don’t believe in points for wines and will never use them for any of my tasting notes.

The reviewer did review the wine 2X, on different occasions and said ‘disappointing’ both times. Conversely, I have reviewed this same wine 6X since release and I love this stuff. 2012 was the second vintage and the wine is going to go along time. I think a lot of Steve, of Thomas and the wines they are making, so here is my most recent note on the 2012.

  • 2012 Riverain Syrah Cardiac Hill Bennett Valley - USA, California, Sonoma County, Bennett Valley (5/21/2016)
    Drinking this from what is probably the worst stem imagined–small, heavy thing with no bowl. So, I’ll have to wait over dinner later to appreciate the aromatic from a proper stem. In terms of the palate, dark, juicy, with meat, pepper, potpourri, iron. All the stuff I look for in syrah. And the whole cluster inclusion works well here. Where this differs from the 2011 is that there is a darker quality, more depth. Taking to dinner later, will update note tomorrow…this coiled up during dinner and even for a palate as mine where I tend to enjoy and absorb structure, it was evident in the last glass. Lots of the beautiful whole cluster accents yet the structure hanging around. What makes me happy about the tension here is that this wine has the color, the fruit and the acidity to go with the softening of that structure over time and really emerge in say 3-4 years all in unison. If you drink now, decant well, as I should have opened this in the morning, not 3 hours before dinner!

Posted from CellarTracker

link_counts.title: 2012 Riverain Syrah Cardiac Hill Bennett Valley, USA, California, Sonoma County, Bennett Valley - CellarTracker

posts.name: Steve Gautier
posts.cooked: This could also show the value of aeration. Not sure if the original reviewer did PnP or if they would have liked it with more air but it sounds like from Frank’s note, it needs air. I have long wished that a drop down for PnP, various decant times and various slow ox times was part of the CT tasting note process. Many include the info in the tasting note section but many don’t.

posts.name: M A T T H A R T L E Y
posts.cooked:
Cameron Hughes:
Todd F r e n c h:

2012 Riverain Syrah Cardiac Hill Bennett Valley
11/22/2016 - > HAVEAPLANZ@YAHOO.COM > DOES NOT LIKE THIS WINE:
Very disappointing effort from TRB

What numeric rating would this person have on such a wine? One he/she does not like, that he/she says is VERY DISAPPOINTING? What number you ask?

87 points

Wonder how many points he/she gives to a wine he/she likes? 138 points?

Well, it would disappointing if you paid $45 for a Parker 94 point wine when you perceive it to be an “87 point” wine…

Parker gives 94 pts to $12 wines, so if that is my metric I would be upset that a 94 point wine cost $45 in the first place.
posts.display_username: M A T T H A R T L E Y

Frankie - my giggle is solely that someone who feels a wine is SO disappointing, then gives it 87 freakin’ points makes no sense, and further outlines the idiocy of the points scale, of which I’m no fan (everybody here knows that)

I like the wine a lot, too, which is why I bought it on BerserkerDay, and have it in my cellar so that such a note would show when I’m checking it out on CT - I prefer it to the Riverain Cabernet, and not only because Steve said I wouldn’t like the Cab (because I do) - I just like the Syrah much more!
